varieties.name: Acorus calamus (Standard Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Introduced sterile triploid (3n=36) from Eurasia, cultivated origin likely Himalayan region, naturalized worldwide since 1600s
varieties.traits: Robust growth 60-100cm tall, bright green sword-shaped leaves 1-2cm wide, single prominent midrib, wavy/crimped edges, sweet aromatic fragrance when crushed
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate rhizome production, spreads 30-50cm annually via vegetative propagation
varieties.availability: Mudbrick Herb Cottage (NSW/QLD), We Know Water Gardens (Australia-wide), Nature India Nursery (ships to QLD)

varieties.name: Acorus calamus 'Variegatus' (Variegated Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Horticultural selection, developed in Europe/Asia, cultivated for ornamental value since 1800s
varieties.traits: Striking variegation with creamy-white and green longitudinal stripes, 60-75cm tall, 45-60cm wide, pink tinge in cool weather, gracefully arching narrow leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Good rhizome spread, forms dense ground cover over time, slower growth than green form
varieties.availability: We Know Water Gardens (Australia), Nursery Warehouse (Brisbane area), widely available ornamental ponds

varieties.name: Acorus calamus 'Argenteostriatus'
varieties.origin: Synonym for 'Variegatus', European horticultural strain, RHS-recommended naming
varieties.traits: Silver-striped variegation, evergreen perennial, 60-90cm tall, 60-120cm wide, green and white longitudinal stripes

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus (Japanese Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Native to wetland areas of Japan, Korea, China, India, Thailand, Myanmar, Philippines, diploid species (2n=18)
varieties.traits: Compact clumping form 15-30cm tall, glossy narrow linear leaves 0.6cm wide, fan-like arrangement, evergreen to semi-evergreen, soft curved foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Slow to moderate spread, forms attractive clumps up to 60cm wide
varieties.availability: Australian Plants Online, Plantmark wholesale (Melbourne/Sydney/Brisbane), widely available

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Ogon' (Golden Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Japanese cultivar selection, developed for ornamental golden foliage, also known as 'Oborzuki' in some regions
varieties.traits: Brilliant golden-yellow leaves (pure yellow in true cultivar, some strains show green striping), 25-30cm tall, 25-30cm wide, upright clumping habit, year-round color retention
varieties.yield_notes: Slow to moderate spread, reliable clump formation
varieties.availability: White House Nursery (Australia), Nursery Warehouse (Brisbane), Plantmark wholesale (Brisbane), Australian Plants Online, widely stocked

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Variegatus' (Variegated Japanese Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Japanese horticultural selection, cultivated for ornamental striped foliage
varieties.traits: Creamy-white and green longitudinal stripes, arching narrow leaves, 25-30cm tall, 30-50cm wide spread, evergreen in mild climates
varieties.yield_notes: Slow spread forming dense groundcover, clumping habit
varieties.availability: White House Nursery (Australia), Australian Plants Online, Plantmark wholesale (Brisbane)

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Pusillus' (Miniature Sweet Flag)
varieties.origin: Dwarf variety from Japan, selected for extremely compact growth, var. pusillus botanical variety
varieties.traits: Tiny growth 2.5-7cm tall, very short rigid glossy green leaves, ultra-compact clumping, fine texture
varieties.yield_notes: Very slow spread, ideal for miniature gardens and terrariums

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Minimus Aureus' (Dwarf Golden Variegated)
varieties.origin: Japanese dwarf cultivar, golden foliage selection, also sold as 'Pusillus Minimus Aureus'
varieties.traits: Petite 10-15cm tall, bright golden-yellow and green variegated blades, dense tufted mat formation, fine grass-like texture
varieties.yield_notes: Slow spreading groundcover, forms colorful carpet in moist areas
varieties.availability: Emerald Coast Growers (US import possible), specialty aquatic nurseries

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Licorice'
varieties.origin: Cultivar selection for unique licorice scent, Asian horticultural development
varieties.traits: Solid green leaves, 30-45cm tall, thicker and heavier foliage than typical gramineus, distinctive strong licorice fragrance
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to good spread, reliable growth
varieties.availability: Specialty herb and water plant nurseries

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Albovariegatus' (Dwarf White Variegated)
varieties.traits: Compact 10-15cm tall maximum, white stripes on green leaves, dwarf clumping form
varieties.yield_notes: Very slow spread, compact habit ideal for small spaces

varieties.name: Vayambu (Traditional Kerala Acorus calamus)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Kerala and South India, tetraploid form (4n=48) traditionally cultivated throughout tropical Asia, documented medicinal use for thousands of years in Ayurvedic tradition, deeply rooted in Kerala culture
varieties.traits: Semi-aquatic aromatic perennial, narrow linear to narrowly ensiform leaves up to 80cm long, glossy bright green, acute apex, amplexicaul base with 20-50cm sheathing, creeping jointed rhizomes 1.3-2.5cm thick, pale to dark brown
varieties.yield_notes: Good rhizome production in waterlogged conditions, harvested for medicinal preparations
varieties.availability: Santhi Online Plants Nursery (Tamil Nadu/Kerala), Kerala Ayurvedic medicine suppliers, NMPB-RCFC Southern Region (Kerala Forest Research Institute, Peechi), Nature India Nursery, local medicinal plant growers

varieties.name: Acorus calamus var. angustatus (Asian Tetraploid)
varieties.origin: Native tetraploid variety (4n=48) throughout tropical and subtropical Asia including India, E Siberia, Japan, Philippines, Indonesia, most common form in Asian tropics
varieties.traits: Morphologically diverse with some forms having narrow leaves (angustatus = narrow), others broad, variable karyotypes, aromatic volatile oil-rich rhizomes
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent rhizome production in tropical wetland conditions, both sexual and asexual reproduction
varieties.availability: Wild collected from Kerala wetlands (conservation concerns), Ayurvedic medicinal plant farms, government medicinal plant nurseries

varieties.name: Diploid Acorus calamus (Low β-asarone Form)
varieties.origin: Native diploid (2n=24) populations found in parts of Himalayas, Mongolia, C Siberia, Northern Himachal Pradesh India, Gilgit-Baltistan
varieties.traits: Fertile diploid with 2-6 prominent veins equally raised (vs single prominent vein in triploid), morphologically similar to tetraploid but cytologically distinct
varieties.yield_notes: Good rhizome and seed production, both reproductive modes functional
varieties.availability: Rare in Kerala lowlands, may be available from high-elevation Western Ghats sources, northern India suppliers

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus (Wild Asian Form)
varieties.origin: Native to wetland areas of India, Thailand, Myanmar, Philippines, China, Japan, Korea - wide temperate to tropical Asian range
varieties.traits: Dense slowly-spreading clump of sword-shaped leaves up to 50cm tall, extensively-branched creeping slender rhizomes, can be evergreen in tropical areas
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate spread, reliable seed and rhizome production
varieties.availability: May be found wild in Kerala wetlands, ornamental water plant nurseries, traditional medicine suppliers

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Ogon'
varieties.availability: Imported ornamental plant nurseries in major Kerala cities (Kochi, Thiruvananthapuram), specialty water garden suppliers

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Variegatus'
varieties.traits: Green leaves with white stripes, 25-30cm tall, arching habit
varieties.availability: Ornamental nurseries in Kerala cities, water garden specialists

varieties.name: Acorus gramineus 'Pusillus'
varieties.origin: Japanese miniature variety, botanical var. pusillus
varieties.traits: Ultra-dwarf 2.5-7cm tall, tiny rigid glossy green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Very slow spread, ideal for miniature landscapes
varieties.availability: Specialty miniature plant nurseries, terrarium suppliers in major cities
